---
title: "Cross-Validation: DB-Path vs Legacy-Path Assessment"
author: "nemascan-sims-nf"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
    embed-resources: true
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 6
params:
  existing_assessment: ""
  db_assessment: ""
  db_dir: ""
execute:
  eval: true
  warning: true
  message: true
---

## 1. Executive Summary

This report compares the **DB-path** QTL assessment against the **legacy R file-based** assessment
to verify that both pipelines produce identical analytical results. The DB-path queries a
Parquet/DuckDB database populated during the pipeline run, while the legacy path uses R scripts
operating on intermediate files. Both paths analyze the same GWA output and should produce
matching QTL detection, interval boundaries, and significance calls.

The report runs 7 validation checks (mirroring `test-assessment_cross_validation.R`), then uses
the `designate_qtl()` / `count_outcomes()` / `calculate_simrep_performance()` pipeline to compute
Power and FDR by method, with visualizations comparing both paths.

:::{.callout-note}
## Known Design Differences

| Aspect | Legacy path | DB path |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `var.exp` / `Simulated.QTL.VarExp` | Computed from genotype matrix | `NA` (no genotype matrix in DB) |
| `algorithm_id` format | `inbred_nopca_EIGEN` | `LMM-EXACT-INBRED_noPCA_EIGEN` |

Both encode the same information; the `normalize_algorithm_id()` helper strips the prefix and
uppercases for joining.
:::

## 2. Setup

```{r}
#| label: setup
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# Source the shared R library (includes sim_performance.R)
Sys.setenv(NEMASCAN_R_LIB = here::here("R"))
source(here::here("R", "setup.R"))
```

```{r}
#| label: paths
# For interactive use, set full paths here:
# existing_assessment_path <- "/full/path/to/simulation_assessment_results.tsv"
# db_assessment_path       <- "/full/path/to/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv"
# db_dir                   <- "/full/path/to/db"

existing_assessment_path <- ""
db_assessment_path       <- ""
db_dir                   <- ""

# Read from Quarto params, fall back to env vars, then interactive vars above
if (!exists("existing_assessment_path") || existing_assessment_path == "") {
  existing_assessment_path <- if (params$existing_assessment != "") {
    params$existing_assessment
  } else {
    Sys.getenv("TEST_EXISTING_ASSESSMENT", unset = "")
  }
}

if (!exists("db_assessment_path") || db_assessment_path == "") {
  db_assessment_path <- if (params$db_assessment != "") {
    params$db_assessment
  } else {
    Sys.getenv("TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT", unset = "")
  }
}

if (!exists("db_dir") || db_dir == "") {
  db_dir <- if (params$db_dir != "") {
    params$db_dir
  } else {
    Sys.getenv("TEST_DB_DIR", unset = "")
  }
}

# Validate
stopifnot(
  "existing_assessment path not set or file missing" =
    existing_assessment_path != "" && file.exists(existing_assessment_path),
  "db_assessment path not set or file missing" =
    db_assessment_path != "" && file.exists(db_assessment_path),
  "db_dir not set or directory missing" =
    db_dir != "" && dir.exists(db_dir)
)
message("Existing assessment: ", existing_assessment_path)
message("DB assessment:       ", db_assessment_path)
message("DB directory:        ", db_dir)
```

```{r}
#| label: theme-and-helpers

# ggplot2 theme for cross-validation plots
theme_xval <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
theme_set(theme_xval)

path_colors <- c("Legacy" = "#E69F00", "DB" = "#56B4E9")
designation_colors <- c(
  "Detected.CV" = "#2ca02c",
  "Missed.CV" = "#d62728",
  "CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval" = "#ff7f0e",
  "False.Discovery" = "#9467bd"
)

# Parse legacy assessment TSV (no header, 35-column schema from Assess_Sims.R)
read_existing_assessment <- function(path) {
  col_names <- c("QTL", "Simulated", "Detected", "CHROM", "POS", "RefAllele",
                  "Frequency", "Effect", "Simulated.QTL.VarExp", "log10p", "significant",
                  "startPOS", "peakPOS", "endPOS",
                  "detected.peak", "BETA", "interval.log10p",
                  "peak_id", "interval_size",
                  "interval.var.exp", "interval.Frequency",
                  "top.hit", "nQTL", "simREP", "h2", "maf",
                  "effect_distribution", "strain_set_id",
                  "mode", "type", "threshold", "algorithm_id",
                  "alpha", "ci_size", "snp_grouping")

  df <- tryCatch(
    data.table::fread(path, header = FALSE, col.names = col_names,
                      sep = "\t", na.strings = c("NA", "")) %>% as.data.frame(),
    error = function(e) {
      data.table::fread(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t",
                        na.strings = c("NA", "")) %>% as.data.frame()
    }
  )

  df$QTL <- as.character(df$QTL)
  df$Simulated <- as.character(df$Simulated)
  df$Detected <- as.character(df$Detected)
  df$startPOS <- as.integer(df$startPOS)
  df$peakPOS <- as.integer(df$peakPOS)
  df$endPOS <- as.integer(df$endPOS)
  df$nQTL <- as.character(df$nQTL)
  df$simREP <- as.character(df$simREP)
  df$h2 <- as.character(df$h2)
  df$maf <- as.character(df$maf)
  df$strain_set_id <- as.character(df$strain_set_id)
  df
}

# Parse DB assessment TSV (no header, 35-column standardized schema)
read_db_assessment <- function(path) {
  col_names <- c("QTL", "Simulated", "Detected", "CHROM", "POS", "RefAllele",
                  "Frequency", "Effect", "Simulated.QTL.VarExp", "log10p", "significant",
                  "BETA", "startPOS", "peakPOS", "endPOS", "detected.peak",
                  "interval.log10p", "interval.var.exp", "interval.Frequency",
                  "peak_id", "interval_size", "top.hit",
                  "nQTL", "simREP", "h2", "maf", "effect_distribution",
                  "strain_set_id", "mode", "type", "threshold", "algorithm_id",
                  "alpha", "ci_size", "snp_grouping")

  df <- tryCatch(
    data.table::fread(path, header = FALSE, col.names = col_names,
                      sep = "\t", na.strings = c("NA", "")) %>% as.data.frame(),
    error = function(e) {
      data.table::fread(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t",
                        na.strings = c("NA", "")) %>% as.data.frame()
    }
  )

  df$QTL <- as.character(df$QTL)
  df$Simulated <- as.character(df$Simulated)
  df$Detected <- as.character(df$Detected)
  df$startPOS <- as.integer(df$startPOS)
  df$peakPOS <- as.integer(df$peakPOS)
  df$endPOS <- as.integer(df$endPOS)
  df$nQTL <- as.character(df$nQTL)
  df$simREP <- as.character(df$simREP)
  df$h2 <- as.character(df$h2)
  df$maf <- as.character(df$maf)
  df$strain_set_id <- as.character(df$strain_set_id)
  df
}

# Normalize algorithm_id across both paths for joining
normalize_algorithm_id <- function(alg_id) {
  toupper(sub("^LMM-EXACT-", "", toupper(alg_id)))
}

# Create a composite key for joining rows across both assessments
make_join_key <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      norm_alg = normalize_algorithm_id(algorithm_id),
      join_key = paste(nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, effect_distribution,
                       strain_set_id, norm_alg, QTL, sep = "|")
    )
}

# Create method label from mode/type/threshold for stratified analysis
parse_method_label <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      norm_alg = normalize_algorithm_id(algorithm_id),
      method_label = paste(
        gsub("_", "/", sub("_[A-Z]+$", "", norm_alg)),  # mode/pca
        sub(".*_", "", norm_alg),                         # threshold
        sep = " "
      )
    )
}

# HTML badge for validation summary
make_badge <- function(passed, label = NULL) {
  color <- if (passed) "#28a745" else "#dc3545"
  text <- if (is.null(label)) (if (passed) "PASS" else "FAIL") else label
  sprintf('<span style="background:%s;color:white;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:4px;font-weight:bold;font-size:0.85em;">%s</span>',
          color, text)
}
```

## 3. Data Loading & Overview

```{r}
#| label: load-data
existing <- read_existing_assessment(existing_assessment_path)
db_assess <- read_db_assessment(db_assessment_path)

message("Existing assessment: ", nrow(existing), " rows x ", ncol(existing), " cols")
message("DB assessment:       ", nrow(db_assess), " rows x ", ncol(db_assess), " cols")
```

```{r}
#| label: designate-and-label
# Apply designate_qtl() from sim_performance.R to classify outcomes
existing_d <- existing %>% designate_qtl() %>% parse_method_label() %>% mutate(path = "Legacy")
db_d <- db_assess %>% designate_qtl() %>% parse_method_label() %>% mutate(path = "DB")
```

```{r}
#| label: data-dimensions
#| results: asis
dim_table <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Rows", "Unique methods", "Threshold types",
             "Detected.CV", "Missed.CV", "CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval", "False.Discovery"),
  Legacy = c(
    nrow(existing_d),
    length(unique(existing_d$method_label)),
    paste(sort(unique(existing_d$threshold)), collapse = ", "),
    sum(existing_d$designation == "Detected.CV", na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(existing_d$designation == "Missed.CV", na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(existing_d$designation == "CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval", na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(existing_d$designation == "False.Discovery", na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  DB = c(
    nrow(db_d),
    length(unique(db_d$method_label)),
    paste(sort(unique(db_d$threshold)), collapse = ", "),
    sum(db_d$designation == "Detected.CV", na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(db_d$designation == "Missed.CV", na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(db_d$designation == "CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval", na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(db_d$designation == "False.Discovery", na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)
knitr::kable(dim_table, caption = "Data Dimensions & Designation Counts")
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Legacy Data

```{r}
#| label: preview-legacy
knitr::kable(head(existing, 5))
```

### DB Data

```{r}
#| label: preview-db
knitr::kable(head(db_assess, 5))
```

:::

## 4. Validation Summary

```{r}
#| label: run-checks

# Check 1: Output files exist and are non-empty
run_file_check <- function() {
  existing_ok <- file.exists(existing_assessment_path) && file.info(existing_assessment_path)$size > 0
  db_ok <- file.exists(db_assessment_path) && file.info(db_assessment_path)$size > 0
  passed <- existing_ok && db_ok
  list(
    passed = passed,
    summary = ifelse(passed, "Both files exist and are non-empty",
                     "One or more files missing or empty"),
    details = sprintf("Legacy: %s (%s bytes), DB: %s (%s bytes)",
                      existing_assessment_path, file.info(existing_assessment_path)$size,
                      db_assessment_path, file.info(db_assessment_path)$size)
  )
}

# Check 2: Row counts per mapping match
run_row_count_check <- function() {
  existing_counts <- existing %>%
    mutate(norm_alg = normalize_algorithm_id(algorithm_id)) %>%
    group_by(nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, strain_set_id, norm_alg) %>%
    summarise(n_existing = n(), .groups = "drop")

  db_counts <- db_assess %>%
    mutate(norm_alg = normalize_algorithm_id(algorithm_id)) %>%
    group_by(nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, strain_set_id, norm_alg) %>%
    summarise(n_db = n(), .groups = "drop")

  merged <- full_join(existing_counts, db_counts,
                      by = c("nQTL", "simREP", "h2", "maf", "strain_set_id", "norm_alg"))
  mismatched <- merged %>% filter(n_existing != n_db | is.na(n_existing) | is.na(n_db))
  passed <- nrow(mismatched) == 0
  list(
    passed = passed,
    summary = ifelse(passed,
                     sprintf("All %d mappings have matching row counts", nrow(merged)),
                     sprintf("%d of %d mappings have mismatched counts", nrow(mismatched), nrow(merged))),
    details = if (!passed) mismatched else NULL
  )
}

# Check 3: Detection outcomes match
run_detection_check <- function() {
  existing_sim <- existing %>% filter(Simulated == "TRUE") %>% make_join_key()
  db_sim <- db_assess %>% filter(Simulated == "TRUE") %>% make_join_key()

  if (nrow(existing_sim) == 0 || nrow(db_sim) == 0) {
    return(list(passed = NA, summary = "No simulated QTLs to compare", details = NULL))
  }

  merged <- inner_join(
    existing_sim %>% select(join_key, Detected_existing = Detected),
    db_sim %>% select(join_key, Detected_db = Detected),
    by = "join_key"
  )
  mismatches <- merged %>% filter(Detected_existing != Detected_db)
  passed <- nrow(mismatches) == 0
  list(
    passed = passed,
    summary = ifelse(passed,
                     sprintf("Detection outcomes match for all %d simulated QTLs", nrow(merged)),
                     sprintf("%d detection mismatches out of %d", nrow(mismatches), nrow(merged))),
    details = if (!passed) mismatches else NULL
  )
}

# Check 4: Threshold values match
run_threshold_check <- function() {
  marker_sets <- list_marker_sets(db_dir)
  if (nrow(marker_sets) == 0) return(list(passed = NA, summary = "No marker sets in DB", details = NULL))

  results <- list()
  all_pass <- TRUE
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(marker_sets))) {
    pop <- marker_sets$population[i]
    maf_val <- marker_sets$maf[i]
    thresh_params <- get_threshold_params(pop, maf_val, 0.05, db_dir)

    expected_bf <- -log10(0.05 / thresh_params$n_markers)
    bf_ok <- isTRUE(all.equal(thresh_params$bf_threshold, expected_bf))

    if (!is.na(thresh_params$n_independent_tests)) {
      expected_eigen <- -log10(0.05 / thresh_params$n_independent_tests)
      eigen_ok <- isTRUE(all.equal(thresh_params$eigen_threshold, expected_eigen))
    } else {
      expected_eigen <- NA
      eigen_ok <- TRUE
    }

    if (!bf_ok || !eigen_ok) all_pass <- FALSE
    results[[i]] <- data.frame(
      population = pop, maf = maf_val,
      bf_actual = round(thresh_params$bf_threshold, 4),
      bf_expected = round(expected_bf, 4), bf_ok = bf_ok,
      eigen_actual = ifelse(is.na(thresh_params$eigen_threshold), NA, round(thresh_params$eigen_threshold, 4)),
      eigen_expected = ifelse(is.na(expected_eigen), NA, round(expected_eigen, 4)),
      eigen_ok = eigen_ok
    )
  }
  list(
    passed = all_pass,
    summary = ifelse(all_pass,
                     sprintf("All thresholds match for %d marker sets", nrow(marker_sets)),
                     "One or more thresholds do not match"),
    details = do.call(rbind, results)
  )
}

# Check 5: QTL interval boundaries match
run_interval_check <- function() {
  existing_detected <- existing %>% filter(Detected == "TRUE", !is.na(startPOS)) %>% make_join_key()
  db_detected <- db_assess %>% filter(Detected == "TRUE", !is.na(startPOS)) %>% make_join_key()

  if (nrow(existing_detected) == 0 || nrow(db_detected) == 0) {
    return(list(passed = NA, summary = "No detected QTLs with intervals to compare", details = NULL))
  }

  merged <- inner_join(
    existing_detected %>% select(join_key, startPOS_e = startPOS, peakPOS_e = peakPOS, endPOS_e = endPOS),
    db_detected %>% select(join_key, startPOS_d = startPOS, peakPOS_d = peakPOS, endPOS_d = endPOS),
    by = "join_key"
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      start_ok = startPOS_e == startPOS_d,
      peak_ok = peakPOS_e == peakPOS_d,
      end_ok = endPOS_e == endPOS_d
    )

  n_bad <- sum(!merged$start_ok) + sum(!merged$peak_ok) + sum(!merged$end_ok)
  passed <- n_bad == 0
  list(
    passed = passed,
    summary = ifelse(passed,
                     sprintf("All interval boundaries match for %d detected QTLs", nrow(merged)),
                     sprintf("%d boundary mismatches across %d QTLs", n_bad, nrow(merged))),
    details = if (!passed) merged %>% filter(!start_ok | !peak_ok | !end_ok) else NULL
  )
}

# Check 6: Detected/simulated counts per mapping match
run_count_check <- function() {
  count_by_mapping <- function(df) {
    df %>%
      mutate(norm_alg = normalize_algorithm_id(algorithm_id)) %>%
      group_by(nQTL, simREP, h2, maf, strain_set_id, norm_alg) %>%
      summarise(
        n_detected = sum(Detected == "TRUE", na.rm = TRUE),
        n_simulated = sum(Simulated == "TRUE", na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      )
  }
  existing_counts <- count_by_mapping(existing)
  db_counts <- count_by_mapping(db_assess)

  merged <- inner_join(existing_counts, db_counts,
                       by = c("nQTL", "simREP", "h2", "maf", "strain_set_id", "norm_alg"),
                       suffix = c("_existing", "_db"))

  detect_bad <- merged %>% filter(n_detected_existing != n_detected_db)
  sim_bad <- merged %>% filter(n_simulated_existing != n_simulated_db)
  passed <- nrow(detect_bad) == 0 && nrow(sim_bad) == 0
  list(
    passed = passed,
    summary = ifelse(passed,
                     sprintf("Detected/simulated counts match across all %d mappings", nrow(merged)),
                     sprintf("%d detected + %d simulated count mismatches", nrow(detect_bad), nrow(sim_bad))),
    details = if (!passed) bind_rows(detect_bad, sim_bad) else NULL
  )
}

# Check 7: var.exp is NA in DB path
run_varexp_check <- function() {
  if (!"interval.var.exp" %in% names(db_assess)) {
    return(list(passed = NA, summary = "interval.var.exp column not present", details = NULL))
  }
  non_na <- sum(!is.na(db_assess$interval.var.exp))
  passed <- non_na == 0
  list(
    passed = passed,
    summary = ifelse(passed,
                     sprintf("interval.var.exp is all NA in DB path (%d rows)", nrow(db_assess)),
                     sprintf("%d rows have non-NA interval.var.exp", non_na)),
    details = NULL
  )
}

# Run all checks
checks <- list(
  "1. Output Files" = run_file_check(),
  "2. Row Counts" = run_row_count_check(),
  "3. Detection Outcomes" = run_detection_check(),
  "4. Threshold Values" = run_threshold_check(),
  "5. Interval Boundaries" = run_interval_check(),
  "6. Detected/Simulated Counts" = run_count_check(),
  "7. var.exp NA in DB" = run_varexp_check()
)
```

```{r}
#| label: validation-summary-table
#| results: asis
summary_rows <- lapply(names(checks), function(nm) {
  chk <- checks[[nm]]
  badge <- if (is.na(chk$passed)) make_badge(TRUE, "SKIP") else make_badge(chk$passed)
  data.frame(Check = nm, Status = badge, Detail = chk$summary, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
})
summary_df <- do.call(rbind, summary_rows)

knitr::kable(summary_df, escape = FALSE, caption = "Validation Summary")
```

```{r}
#| label: check-details
#| results: asis
# Show details for any failed checks
for (nm in names(checks)) {
  chk <- checks[[nm]]
  if (!is.null(chk$details) && !isTRUE(chk$passed)) {
    cat(sprintf("\n### %s - Details\n\n", nm))
    print(knitr::kable(chk$details))
    cat("\n")
  }
}
```

## 5. Method-Level Cross-Path Comparison

### 5.1 Detection Designations by Method

```{r}
#| label: designation-by-method
# Combine both paths for comparison
combined <- bind_rows(existing_d, db_d)

# Count designations per method and path
desig_counts <- combined %>%
  filter(!is.na(designation)) %>%
  group_by(method_label, path, designation) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")
```

```{r}
#| label: viz-1-designation-bars
#| fig-cap: "Detection designations by method — DB vs Legacy"
ggplot(desig_counts,
       aes(x = designation, y = n, fill = path)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = path_colors) +
  facet_wrap(~ method_label, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Designation", y = "Count", fill = "Path") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1, size = 8))
```

```{r}
#| label: viz-2-agreement-heatmap
#| fig-cap: "Per-QTL designation agreement between paths"
#| eval: !expr nrow(existing_d) <= 200

# Join designations by QTL key
existing_desig <- existing_d %>% make_join_key() %>% select(join_key, method_label, designation_legacy = designation)
db_desig <- db_d %>% make_join_key() %>% select(join_key, designation_db = designation)

agreement <- inner_join(existing_desig, db_desig, by = "join_key") %>%
  filter(!is.na(designation_legacy), !is.na(designation_db))

if (nrow(agreement) > 0) {
  agree_matrix <- agreement %>%
    count(designation_legacy, designation_db) %>%
    tidyr::complete(designation_legacy, designation_db, fill = list(n = 0))

  ggplot(agree_matrix, aes(x = designation_legacy, y = designation_db, fill = n)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = n), size = 5) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#2ca02c") +
    labs(x = "Legacy Designation", y = "DB Designation", fill = "Count") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))
}
```

### 5.2 Power & FDR Comparison by Method

```{r}
#| label: power-fdr-by-method
# Compute performance per method and path using sim_performance pipeline
perf_by_method <- combined %>%
  filter(!is.na(designation)) %>%
  group_by(method_label, path) %>%
  group_modify(~ count_outcomes(.x)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  calculate_simrep_performance(stringent = FALSE)

perf_by_method_stringent <- combined %>%
  filter(!is.na(designation)) %>%
  group_by(method_label, path) %>%
  group_modify(~ count_outcomes(.x)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  calculate_simrep_performance(stringent = TRUE) %>%
  rename(Power_stringent = Power, FDR_stringent = FDR)
```

```{r}
#| label: viz-3-power-comparison
#| fig-cap: "Power by method — Legacy vs DB"
ggplot(perf_by_method, aes(x = method_label, y = Power, fill = path)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = path_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), labels = percent_format()) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.4) +
  labs(x = "Method", y = "Power", fill = "Path") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
#| label: viz-4-fdr-comparison
#| fig-cap: "False Discovery Rate by method — Legacy vs DB"
ggplot(perf_by_method, aes(x = method_label, y = FDR, fill = path)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = path_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA), labels = percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "Method", y = "FDR", fill = "Path") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
#| label: power-fdr-table
#| results: asis
perf_table <- perf_by_method %>%
  select(method_label, path, Detected.CV, Missed.CV, CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval,
         False.Discovery, Power, FDR) %>%
  mutate(Power = sprintf("%.1f%%", Power * 100), FDR = sprintf("%.1f%%", FDR * 100))
knitr::kable(perf_table, caption = "Power & FDR by Method and Path")
```

### 5.3 Interval Boundaries by Method

```{r}
#| label: interval-boundary-data
existing_detected <- existing_d %>%
  filter(Detected == "TRUE", !is.na(startPOS)) %>%
  make_join_key()

db_detected <- db_d %>%
  filter(Detected == "TRUE", !is.na(startPOS)) %>%
  make_join_key()

interval_merged <- if (nrow(existing_detected) > 0 && nrow(db_detected) > 0) {
  inner_join(
    existing_detected %>%
      select(join_key, method_label,
             startPOS_legacy = startPOS, peakPOS_legacy = peakPOS, endPOS_legacy = endPOS,
             interval_size_legacy = interval_size),
    db_detected %>%
      select(join_key,
             startPOS_db = startPOS, peakPOS_db = peakPOS, endPOS_db = endPOS,
             interval_size_db = interval_size),
    by = "join_key"
  )
} else {
  data.frame()
}
```

```{r}
#| label: viz-5-boundary-scatter
#| fig-cap: "Interval boundary positions — Legacy vs DB (diagonal = perfect match)"
#| eval: !expr nrow(interval_merged) > 0

boundary_long <- interval_merged %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(startPOS_legacy, peakPOS_legacy, endPOS_legacy),
    names_to = "boundary_type_legacy",
    values_to = "pos_legacy"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    boundary_type = gsub("POS_legacy", "", boundary_type_legacy),
    pos_db = case_when(
      boundary_type == "start" ~ startPOS_db,
      boundary_type == "peak" ~ peakPOS_db,
      boundary_type == "end" ~ endPOS_db
    )
  )

ggplot(boundary_long, aes(x = pos_legacy, y = pos_db, color = method_label)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ boundary_type, scales = "free") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  labs(x = "Legacy Position (bp)", y = "DB Position (bp)", color = "Method")
```

```{r}
#| label: viz-6-interval-sizes
#| fig-cap: "Interval sizes — Legacy vs DB per detected QTL"
#| eval: !expr nrow(interval_merged) > 0

n_detected <- nrow(interval_merged)

if (n_detected <= 50) {
  # Dumbbell chart for small N
  interval_dumbbell <- interval_merged %>%
    mutate(qtl_id = row_number()) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = c(interval_size_legacy, interval_size_db),
      names_to = "path_raw",
      values_to = "interval_size"
    ) %>%
    mutate(path = ifelse(grepl("legacy", path_raw), "Legacy", "DB"))

  ggplot(interval_dumbbell, aes(x = interval_size / 1e3, y = reorder(as.factor(qtl_id), interval_size), color = path)) +
    geom_line(aes(group = qtl_id), color = "grey60") +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    scale_color_manual(values = path_colors) +
    labs(x = "Interval Size (kb)", y = "Detected QTL", color = "Path")
} else {
  # Boxplot for large N
  interval_box <- interval_merged %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = c(interval_size_legacy, interval_size_db),
      names_to = "path_raw",
      values_to = "interval_size"
    ) %>%
    mutate(path = ifelse(grepl("legacy", path_raw), "Legacy", "DB"))

  ggplot(interval_box, aes(x = path, y = interval_size / 1e3, fill = path)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = path_colors) +
    facet_wrap(~ method_label) +
    labs(x = "Path", y = "Interval Size (kb)", fill = "Path")
}
```

### 5.4 Log10p Comparison by Method

```{r}
#| label: viz-7-log10p-scatter
#| fig-cap: "Log10p for simulated QTLs — Legacy vs DB"

existing_log10p <- existing_d %>%
  filter(Simulated == "TRUE") %>%
  make_join_key() %>%
  select(join_key, method_label, log10p_legacy = log10p, Detected_legacy = Detected)

db_log10p <- db_d %>%
  filter(Simulated == "TRUE") %>%
  make_join_key() %>%
  select(join_key, log10p_db = log10p, Detected_db = Detected)

log10p_merged <- inner_join(existing_log10p, db_log10p, by = "join_key")

if (nrow(log10p_merged) > 0) {
  log10p_merged <- log10p_merged %>%
    mutate(agreement = case_when(
      Detected_legacy == Detected_db ~ "Agree",
      TRUE ~ "Disagree"
    ))

  ggplot(log10p_merged, aes(x = log10p_legacy, y = log10p_db, color = method_label, shape = agreement)) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("Agree" = 16, "Disagree" = 4)) +
    labs(x = "Legacy log10p", y = "DB log10p", color = "Method", shape = "Detection")
} else {
  message("No simulated QTLs could be joined between paths.")
}
```

## 6. Cross-Method Comparison

This section uses the **DB-path** data as reference (since both paths should be identical, either can serve as the reference).

### 6.1 Detection Power Across Methods

```{r}
#| label: cross-method-performance
# Performance per method using DB path
db_perf <- db_d %>%
  filter(!is.na(designation)) %>%
  group_by(method_label) %>%
  group_modify(~ count_outcomes(.x)) %>%
  ungroup()

db_power <- db_perf %>% calculate_simrep_performance(stringent = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(type = "Non-stringent")
db_power_strict <- db_perf %>% calculate_simrep_performance(stringent = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(type = "Stringent") %>%
  rename(Power_s = Power) %>%
  select(method_label, Power_s)

db_power_combined <- db_power %>%
  left_join(db_power_strict, by = "method_label")
```

```{r}
#| label: viz-8-power-by-method
#| fig-cap: "Power by method (DB path) — stringent vs non-stringent"
power_long <- db_power_combined %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Power, Power_s),
    names_to = "power_type",
    values_to = "power_value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(power_type = ifelse(power_type == "Power", "Non-stringent", "Stringent"))

ggplot(power_long, aes(x = power_value, y = reorder(method_label, power_value), fill = power_type)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7), width = 0.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), labels = percent_format()) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Non-stringent" = "#56B4E9", "Stringent" = "#0072B2")) +
  labs(x = "Power", y = "Method", fill = "Definition")
```

```{r}
#| label: viz-9-fdr-by-method
#| fig-cap: "False Discovery Rate by method (DB path)"
ggplot(db_power, aes(x = FDR, y = reorder(method_label, FDR))) +
  geom_col(fill = "#d62728", width = 0.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, NA), labels = percent_format()) +
  labs(x = "FDR", y = "Method")
```

### 6.2 Interval Precision Across Methods

```{r}
#| label: viz-10-interval-precision
#| fig-cap: "Interval size distribution by threshold type"
db_detected_all <- db_d %>%
  filter(Detected == "TRUE", !is.na(interval_size))

if (nrow(db_detected_all) > 0) {
  ggplot(db_detected_all, aes(x = threshold, y = as.numeric(interval_size) / 1e3, fill = threshold)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
    facet_wrap(~ paste(mode, type, sep = "/"), scales = "free_y") +
    labs(x = "Threshold Type", y = "Interval Size (kb)", fill = "Threshold")
} else {
  message("No detected QTLs with interval sizes to plot.")
}
```

### 6.3 Summary Table

```{r}
#| label: cross-method-summary
#| results: asis
if (nrow(db_power_combined) > 0) {
  # Get interval sizes per method
  interval_summary <- db_d %>%
    filter(Detected == "TRUE", !is.na(interval_size)) %>%
    group_by(method_label) %>%
    summarise(mean_interval_mb = mean(as.numeric(interval_size), na.rm = TRUE) / 1e6, .groups = "drop")

  summary_table <- db_power_combined %>%
    left_join(interval_summary, by = "method_label") %>%
    mutate(
      Power_pct = sprintf("%.1f%%", Power * 100),
      Power_s_pct = sprintf("%.1f%%", Power_s * 100),
      FDR_pct = sprintf("%.1f%%", FDR * 100),
      mean_interval_mb = ifelse(is.na(mean_interval_mb), "-", sprintf("%.3f", mean_interval_mb))
    ) %>%
    select(
      Method = method_label,
      `N Simulated` = Simulated,
      Detected.CV, Missed.CV, CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval, False.Discovery,
      Power = Power_pct, `Power (stringent)` = Power_s_pct,
      FDR = FDR_pct, `Mean Interval (Mb)` = mean_interval_mb
    )

  knitr::kable(summary_table, caption = "Cross-Method Performance Summary (DB Path)")
}
```

## 7. Threshold Validation

```{r}
#| label: threshold-detail
#| results: asis
thresh_check <- checks[["4. Threshold Values"]]
if (!is.null(thresh_check$details)) {
  knitr::kable(thresh_check$details, caption = "Threshold Validation Details")
}
```

## 8. Appendix

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Full Joined Comparison

```{r}
#| label: appendix-full-join
existing_keyed <- existing_d %>% make_join_key()
db_keyed <- db_d %>% make_join_key()

full_comparison <- inner_join(
  existing_keyed %>%
    select(join_key, method_label, designation_legacy = designation,
           log10p_legacy = log10p, Detected_legacy = Detected,
           startPOS_legacy = startPOS, peakPOS_legacy = peakPOS, endPOS_legacy = endPOS),
  db_keyed %>%
    select(join_key, designation_db = designation,
           log10p_db = log10p, Detected_db = Detected,
           startPOS_db = startPOS, peakPOS_db = peakPOS, endPOS_db = endPOS),
  by = "join_key"
)

knitr::kable(head(full_comparison, 50), caption = "Joined comparison (first 50 rows)")
```

### Column Schema

```{r}
#| label: appendix-columns
data.frame(
  col_index = seq_along(names(existing)),
  existing = names(existing),
  db = names(db_assess),
  same = names(existing) == names(db_assess)
) %>% knitr::kable(caption = "Column name comparison")
```

:::
